reasoning: The Lean definition `countably_compact X` quantifies over sequences `U : ℕ → Set X` of opens covering the universe and asserts the existence of a finite subcover — equivalent to "every countable open cover has a finite subcover" since any countable collection can be enumerated by ℕ with repetition. The definition `limit_point_compact_strong X` says every infinite subset U has some x with `ClusterPt x (𝓟 (U \ {x}))`, which unfolds to: every neighborhood of x meets U \ {x} — exactly Munkres's "x is a limit point of U." The theorem's hypothesis `T1Space X` is the standard T1 axiom, and the conclusion is the iff asserted in the exercise. All quantifier structures, types, and mathematical objects match the informal claim.
